CodeBreaK 200: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study of Sotorasib vs Docetaxel

ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT04303780
Sponsor: Amgen
Phase: Phase 3
Condition: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) with KRAS G12C Mutation
Enrollment: 345 participants
Status: Active, Not Recruiting
Start Date: 2020-06-04
Completion Date: 2026-05-29

This trial evaluates the efficacy and safety of sotorasib (AMG 510) in patients
with KRAS G12C-mutated NSCLC. Comparator arm receives docetaxel. Supportive
care may include ibuprofen for pain management and remdesivir if concurrent
COVID-19 infection is identified. Trial registration on ClinicalTrials.gov as
NCT04303780.

Primary Endpoint: Progression-free survival.
Secondary Endpoints: Overall response rate, overall survival, safety.

Study Design:
- Randomized 1:1 allocation
- Open-label design
- Multi-center international study
- Approximately 345 patients with KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC who have received
  at least one prior systemic therapy

Intervention:
- Arm A: Sotorasib (AMG 510) 960 mg orally once daily
- Arm B: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 21 days

Eligibility Criteria:
- Adults with confirmed KRAS G12C-mutated NSCLC
- Received prior platinum-based chemotherapy and anti-PD-1/L1 therapy
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function

Sponsor Contact: Amgen Inc., Thousand Oaks, California
